pattern: Walking prefix sums left to right while remembering the shortest target-sum window ending at or before each position lets every new window instantly pair up with the best compatible partner to its left.
hints: A subarray with sum == target ending at index r can be found in O(1) with a hashmap of prefix sums: it exists whenever prefixSum[r] - target has appeared before.
Keep a running array best[i] = the shortest target-sum subarray that ends anywhere at or before index i (or infinity if none yet). It only ever needs the value from one position back.

Whenever you find a target-sum window ending at r starting right after some index l, the best combined answer using that window is its length plus best[l] - update the answer with that sum, then refresh best[r].

edge_inputs.description: three single-element matches, pick any two for the minimal combined length
matches of different lengths, minimal combined sum mixes a short and a longer one
single element array can never supply two disjoint subarrays
target exceeds the total sum, so no subarray at all can reach it
overlapping windows of the same target sum must not both be chosen
a longer early match would beat later shorter ones only if overlap were ignored
many equal-sum windows of varying length require tracking the true minimum seen so far, not just the most recent

stress.description: near-max length with small values and a mid-range target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 3000)
    return [arr, target]

stress.description: dense small values so many overlapping target-sum windows exist
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 4) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(1, 8)
    return [arr, target]
